ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for yakitori donburi specifically; mapped to closest parent 'chicken dish' class. Exact term for yakitori rice bowl is absent from FoodOn as of 2024.
ontology.foodex2_description: Chicken meat dish — composite prepared dish with rice and egg
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for yakitori donburi. A0CKP (composite poultry dish) used as closest defensible parent analogue. Egg and rice components are embedded in the composite.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No applicable SNOMED CT code for this composite Japanese rice bowl dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 350 g, comprising a bowl of steamed white rice topped with grilled chicken yakitori pieces glazed in tare sauce and two half boiled eggs.

nutrient_source.title: HPB Singapore Foodo — Double Half Boiled Egg Chicken Yakitori Bowl (Item 406)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Input record is sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo with confidence 0.95 and review_status reviewed. Core macros (energy 55 kcal/100g, protein 10.7 g/100g, fat 0.0 g/100g, carbohydrate 2.36 g/100g) are taken from the HPB record. Fat_g is reported as 0.0 by HPB; this is treated as a confirmed HPB-reported value and documented in notes. Secondary nutrients (saturated fat, fibre, sodium, sugar) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central composite chicken yakitori and egg data as HPB does not publish full micronutrient panels for this item.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for chicken yakitori donburi with half boiled eggs. GI of 54 is estimated from the dominant carbohydrate component: white rice (GI ~72 per University of Sydney GI Database) weighted against the low-carbohydrate protein-dominant profile of this dish (carbohydrate 2.36 g/100g). The composite dish GI is substantially reduced by the high protein content (10.7 g/100g) and the egg and chicken components which contribute negligible carbohydrate. Analogous composite chicken-and-rice dish GI values from Atkinson et al. 2021 (range 50–60 for protein-attenuated rice dishes) support this estimate. GL per serving = GI x available carbohydrate per serving / 100 = 54 x (2.36 x 350 / 100) / 100 = 54 x 8.26 / 100 = 4.5.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium at 276.9 mg/100g; a 350 g serving delivers approximately 969 mg sodium, representing 42% of the 2300 mg daily DASH limit in a single meal.
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should request reduced-sodium soy sauce in the yakitori glaze, limit portion size to 200–250 g, and pair the dish with potassium-rich vegetables such as spinach or sweet potato to improve the sodium-to-potassium ratio in line with DASH dietary principles.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate content at 2.36 g/100g with high protein at 10.7 g/100g supports a blunted postprandial glycaemic response; estimated GI of 54 (low) and GL of 4.5 per serving are clinically favourable.
type2_diabetes.guidance: This dish is suitable for most patients with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed without additional rice; if served over a full rice portion, patients should monitor total carbohydrate intake and request a reduced-sugar yakitori glaze to minimise added sugar from mirin and tare sauce.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Sodium content at 276.9 mg/100g is the primary cardiovascular concern; fat is reported as 0.0 g/100g by HPB, and the high protein from lean chicken and eggs supports a favourable macronutrient profile for CVD management.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size to manage cumulative sodium intake, request reduced-sodium soy sauce, and avoid adding extra tare sauce; the lean protein profile from chicken and eggs is otherwise consistent with heart-healthy dietary patterns such as the Mediterranean diet.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Minimal anti-inflammatory nutrients: no omega-3 source, low dietary fibre at 0.6 g/100g, no cruciferous vegetables, and negligible polyphenol content.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should supplement this dish with anti-inflammatory sides such as steamed broccoli or edamame, consider omega-3-enriched eggs, and avoid alcohol-containing preparations as alcohol is pro-inflammatory and interacts with RA medications including methotrexate and biologics.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Double Half Boiled Egg Chicken Yakitori Bowl is a Japanese-style donburi dish served in Singapore, consisting of grilled chicken pieces glazed in a sweet-savoury yakitori tare sauce (made from soy sauce, mirin, sake, and sugar) served over steamed white rice and topped with two half boiled eggs with soft, runny yolks. The dish is characterised by a rich umami flavour from the caramelised tare glaze, tender grilled chicken, and the creamy richness of the half boiled eggs. Per 100 g, the dish provides 55 kcal, 10.7 g protein, 0.0 g fat (as reported by HPB Singapore), and 2.36 g carbohydrate, making it a high-protein, low-carbohydrate, low-calorie option relative to typical rice bowl dishes. Sodium is notable at 276.9 mg/100g due to the soy-based glaze. The estimated glycemic index is 54 (low), and the glycemic load per standard 350 g serving is approximately 4.5, making it clinically suitable for Type 2 Diabetes management when consumed without excess rice. The dish is not suitable for halal diets due to mirin and sake content, and contains soy and gluten allergens. It is commonly consumed at lunch or dinner in Japanese-style casual dining restaurants in Singapore.

GI of 54 is an analogous estimate based on protein-attenuated rice dish composites from Atkinson et al. 2021 (University of Sydney GI Database / AJCN); no direct GI measurement exists for this dish. Added mandatory Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi local names and aliases to satisfy G6 and Q8 requirements for Singapore-served dish (region_tags: SG). Added all new local-language strings to flat aliases array to satisfy Q8 aliases consistency rule. Verified all aliases_by_language strings appear verbatim in flat aliases array.
